skill.name: calculator
skill.description: Performs basic arithmetic and expression calculations for AI-assisted math tasks.
skill.summary: Calculate arithmetic expressions with addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, powers, and parentheses.
skill.author: AI Creator

security_audit.summary: The static eval finding is a true positive: calculator.py evaluates a caller-provided expression after a regex filter. The Markdown backtick and weak-crypto findings are false positives because they point to operator examples and descriptive text, not shell execution or cryptographic code. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, or data exfiltration evidence was found in the reviewed files.

high_findings.title: User-Controlled eval() in Calculator Helper
high_findings.description: calculator.py calls eval() on a user-supplied expression. The character filter and empty builtins reduce direct code execution risk, but eval still creates a dangerous execution surface and can allow resource exhaustion through very large arithmetic expressions.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The eval() call is direct evidence of dynamic evaluation on caller-controlled input. The surrounding regex limits the input language, so the concern is dangerous evaluation and denial of service rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to Markdown code fences, inline operator text, and example calculations. No shell command invocation, Ruby execution, or command injection vector was found in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation formatting and arithmetic examples only. They do not contain executable shell syntax outside Markdown prose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detection points to the skill description in SKILL.md. That line describes mathematical calculation support and contains no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata text and has no cryptographic API, algorithm name, or security-sensitive operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Expression Evaluation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper uses eval() to compute arithmetic expressions. A safer parser or AST-based evaluator should replace dynamic evaluation before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risky API is present at the cited line. Context confirms it processes expressions supplied to safe_calculate().

content.user_title: Calculate Math Expressions in AI Workflows
content.value_statement: AI assistants often need quick arithmetic checks during planning, writing, or analysis. This skill provides a focused calculator workflow for basic expressions and documented examples.
actual_capabilities: Calculates addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division expressions.
Supports parentheses for grouped arithmetic operations.
Supports exponent notation using either caret or double-asterisk syntax.
Documents example calculations for common arithmetic tasks.
Returns clear errors for unsupported characters and division by zero.
limitations: Uses dynamic eval() internally and should be replaced before public deployment.
Does not support variables, named constants, or custom functions.
Does not provide symbolic algebra, graphing, or unit conversion.
May be unsafe for very large expressions that consume excessive resources.

use_cases.title: Check Quick Arithmetic
use_cases.description: Calculate totals, percentages, or grouped expressions while drafting a response.

use_cases.title: Verify Documentation Examples
use_cases.description: Confirm simple numeric examples before adding them to guides or tutorials.

use_cases.title: Support Coding Tasks
use_cases.description: Evaluate small numeric expressions during estimates, tests, or configuration work.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Codex or Claude Code

prompt_templates.title: Basic Calculation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the calculator skill to solve this expression: 24 + 18 / 3.

prompt_templates.title: Grouped Expression
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate this grouped expression and explain the order of operations: (12 + 8) * 3.

prompt_templates.title: Power Calculation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the calculator skill to compare 2 ^ 8 and 10 ** 2.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Several Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Check these example results and report any mismatch: 5 + 3 = 8, 100 / 4 = 25, and (2 + 3) * 4 = 20.

output_examples.output: The result is 16 because the expression inside parentheses is evaluated first.
The calculation fails because division by zero is not allowed.

best_practices: Use this skill for short arithmetic expressions with numbers and standard operators.
Review results manually when calculations affect financial, legal, or safety decisions.
Replace eval() with a safe expression parser before publishing or deploying this skill.
anti_patterns: Do not pass untrusted long expressions into the current Python helper.
Do not use the skill for algebra, calculus, or symbolic math tasks.
Do not treat Markdown examples as executable shell commands.

faq.question: What operations does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, powers, and parentheses.

faq.question: Can it use variables or functions?
faq.answer: No. The skill documentation states that variables and function calls are not supported.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish as written?
faq.answer: No. The Python helper uses eval(), so it should be rewritten before publication.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Why were Markdown examples flagged by static analysis?
faq.answer: The scanner misread Markdown backticks and arithmetic operators as command execution patterns.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
